query: Federal Reserve Eccles Building renovation federal probe Jerome Powell 2026

b_issues: Same limitation as A: all sources are from Jan 2026, describing only the investigation's launch/backstory, with no mention of the March 2026 resolution (no evidence found, subpoenas quashed) that the anchor's CNN/AP results emphasize. Facts presented (cost, subpoenas, political context) are accurate and sourced from reputable outlets (Axios, NYT, NBC, Bloomberg, Business Insider).
reasoning: Both content sets are essentially frozen at the same point in the timeline (the January 2026 investigation launch) and provide accurate, well-sourced background on the DOJ probe, the $2.5B renovation cost, and the Trump-Powell conflict. Neither set includes the more recent March 2026 development—prosecutor admitting lack of evidence and subpoenas being quashed—which the anchor suggests is a key, more current fact. Since both omit this crucial update equally and otherwise provide comparable, accurate coverage from reliable sources, neither is clearly superior.
